title: fix: normalize finish_reason to OpenAI format
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Lost in translation no more
gist.blurb: We've tightened up our API responses so every model speaks perfect OpenAI. No more weird finish reasons leaking through from different providers—just smooth, standard stops and tool calls.
gist.summary: Normalized API response formats across all text models to ensure strict OpenAI compliance for finish_reason values.
gist.impact: Developers using standard OpenAI SDKs will no longer encounter provider-specific finish reasons like 'end_turn' or 'tool_use', ensuring seamless drop-in integration.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about standardizing messy, varied outputs into one clean format. Polly the bee and the CRT monitor robot working together to sort differently shaped, messy blocks (representing provider quirks) into neat, uniform square boxes (OpenAI standard).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky CRT monitor robot sorting messy, differently shaped blocks into neat, uniform square boxes. The robot's screen glows with a bright green checkmark. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Text in pixel font: 'FORMATTED'. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
